description:
You are a hazmat technician who has arrived at a train derailment. A ruptured tank car has a visible orange placard showing the UN number 1092. Before approaching the spill zone, you need to identify this chemical and compile a quick-reference safety report using the CAMEO Chemicals website.

Create a report file at ~/Documents/un1092_field_report.txt with the following key-value format (one item per line), using values sourced directly from the CAMEO Chemicals database for UN 1092:

Chemical Name: [name from CAMEO]
CAS Number: [CAS registry number]
UN/NA Number: 1092
DOT Hazard Class: [hazard class]
ERG Guide Number: [guide number]
NFPA Health: [rating 0-4]
NFPA Fire: [rating 0-4]
NFPA Instability: [rating 0-4]
Boiling Point: [value with units]
Flash Point: [value with units]
Vapor Density: [value]
Molecular Weight: [value]
Chemical Formula: [formula]
